activated_rules.rule_name: PDF with multiple documents; select correct certificate-invoice pair by invoice reference and original certificate priority
Consignor may be verified if company name appears anywhere in invoice documents
Consignee may be identified in alternative address fields
Goods description verified primarily by article/item numbers when present
When multiple quantity entries exist in certificate, at least one must be verified; explicit contradiction causes mismatch
Single common origin in invoice is sufficient

consignor.motivation: Original certificate on page 1 names consignor 'Components ... Sweden'. The selected invoice (Document A) contains the exact company name 'Components' in the page 1 header/address block together with 'Sweden'. Under rule 4.1.0, explicit occurrence anywhere in the invoice documents is sufficient even though the invoice issuer is another company.

consignee.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Bluesafe, Coenecoop 3D2, 2741PG Waddinxveen, Netherlands' is explicitly present in the invoice buyer/address block and delivery address, with only spacing variation in the postal code.

goods_description.motivation: The certificate lists item identifiers 6402911000, 6403400000, 6403911690, 6403999390 and 6404199000. These exact identifiers are explicitly present in the invoice section headers. Because article numbers are present in the certificate, rule 4.3.2 requires primary verification by article number, which succeeds. The invoice contains two product groups under 6404199000, consistent with the certificate listing two descriptions under the same article number.

quantity.certificate_value:
296 pair
185 pair
968 pair
464 pair
257 pair
86 pair
quantity.motivation: Certificate quantities include explicit numeric values and unit 'pair'. Invoice explicitly shows matching totals 296, 185, 968 and 464 for the first four article groups. For article 6404199000, the certificate splits the quantity into 257 and 86 pairs for two descriptions; the invoice contains the same article number on multiple rows whose quantities sum to 257 for 'STORM Veiligheidsschoen Zwart/Ge...' and 86 for 'STORM Hoge Veiligheidsschoen Zwa...' within the same invoice, satisfying exception 3D.

origin.invoice_value:
6402911000lk Sri Lanka
6403400000lk Sri Lanka
6403911690lk Sri Lanka
6403999390lk Sri Lanka
6404199000lk Sri Lanka
origin.motivation: The certificate states origin 'Sri Lanka'. The invoice explicitly states 'Sri Lanka' repeatedly in the goods sections for the listed products. This is an explicit origin indication and verifies the certificate origin.

overall_assessment.decision_summary: The correct pair is the ORIGINAL certificate and the invoice containing referenced item/order information. All applicable control points are verified under the rules: consignor is explicitly present in the invoice documents, consignee matches, goods are verified by article numbers, quantities are verified directly and by permitted same-invoice row summation for the split 6404199000 entries, and origin 'Sri Lanka' is explicitly stated in the invoice.
